content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
March 13, 1989 
Phillip K. Nails 
Phillip K. Nails and Associates 
920 20th street, Suite 122 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Nails: 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-88-482 
You have requested advice on behalf of your client, Jean Fink 
of the California Cosmotology Association, regarding the cam~aign 
disclosure requirements of the Political Reform Act of 1974. 
QUESTIONS 
1. 
May the California Cosmotology Association add $2.00 to 
the price of its tickets for certain Association-sponsored 
activities which would go to support the Association's political 
committee? 
2. 
Is the Association merely an intermediary for those funds 
when it collects the $2.00 and transmits them to the political 
committee? 
3. 
May the political committee' use the funds it collects 
from the Association in the above-mentioned manner for overhead 
costs as well as to make contributions to candidates? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. 
As long as persons paying to attend Association-sponsored 
activities are informed that $2.00 of their payment will be given 
to the Association's political committee, there is no prohibition 
against fundraising in the way you described. 
2. 
The Association is acting as an intermediary when it 
collects $2.00 per attendee of an activity and transmits the sum 
to the political committee. 
The Association must be listed on the 
political committee's reporting form (Form 420) as an intermediary 
of these funds. 
IGovernment Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to 
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


Phillip Nails 
Page Two 
3. 
As long as the political committee does not receive 
contributions in excess of $2,500 from anyone contributor during 
a fiscal year, the political committee may use the funds collected 
from the Association for overhead expenses or for making 
contributions. 
FACTS 
The California Cosmotology Association sponsors activities at 
which attendees are charged a fee. 
The Association wants to add 
$2.00 to the fee for the purpose of raising contributions for its 
sponsored political committee. 
ANALYSIS 
There are no statutes or regulations which would prohibit the 
association from adding $2.00 to its entrance fee for any event it 
sponsored for the purpose of transmitting the sum amount to its 
sponsored political committee. 
The political committee is required to disclose the 
association as an intermediary of funds transmitted by the 
association on its campaign disclosure statement if the amount 
transmitted is $100 or more. 
No person attending the activities 
need be itemized on the political committee's report unless such 
person contributes $100 or more to the political committee in a 
. 
calendar year. 
(Section 84302.) 
There are no statutes or regulations which would prohibit the 
political committee from using the funds it receives from the 
association for overhead and for making contributions to 
candidates, as long as the political committee does not receive a 
contribution exceeding $2,500 from a single contributor in a 
fiscal year. 
(Section 85302.) 
If the political committee chooses to raise more than $2,500 
from a single contributor in a fiscal year to support its overhead 
expenses, it must establish a bank account separate from the 
account from which it makes contributions to candidates. 
If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5662. 
By: 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. G 
~
en :al C unsel 
. 
~ 
e 
n s.oBraaten-Moen 
F6litical Reform Consultant 
/ 
,/-


~ 
DmI! 
Phillip K. Nails & Associates 
December 23, 1988 
Mr. Kevin Braaten-Moen 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 700 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Kevin, 
Os 
Thank you for meeting with me last week. 
I am authorized by 
Mrs. Jean Fink, President, of the California Cosmotology 
Association to request information from the Commission on their 
behalf. 
We would like confirmation that it is acceptable to the Commis-
sion for the Association to add a $2.00 increase to tickets for 
certain future Association -
sponsored activities for political 
action purposes. 
We understand that if a member or firm purchases 
more than $100.00 of tickets (whether or not for redistribution) 
it will be necessary for us to list donor, occupation, address 
and employer. 
Further we understand you would like us to print 
a notice on tickets saying $2.00 is going to be used for political 
action purposes. 
Two other items we would like confirmed are: 
1.) 
that the Association is merely an intermediary when trans-
ferring $2.00 per person per event to the existing PAC. 
2.) 
that the PAC may use these funds to fund overhead costs as 
well as to make contributions. 
Thank you for your help in this matter. 
S 
cc: 
Representative 
Assoc 
Ken Hitchcock, Legislative Chairman 
California Cosmotology Association 
920 20th Street, Suite 122 • Sacramento, CA 95814 '" (916) 447-3316
